task_completion.description: Access the medical record for patient John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health interface displays the patient record, health evaluation, or demographics screen specifically for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Document the exposure or needlestick diagnosis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagnosis entry indicating 'needlestick', 'exposure', or a relevant ICD-10 code is visible in the patient's conditions, evaluations, or disease profile.

task_completion.description: Order at least two baseline serology laboratory tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A lab order or test request screen showing at least two relevant serology tests (e.g., HIV, Hepatitis B, Hepatitis C) ordered for John Zenon.
A prescription or medication order screen showing an antiretroviral drug (e.g., Zidovudine, Lamivudine, Tenofovir, or other antiviral) prescribed to the patient.

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment 28 to 35 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment record for John Zenon showing a scheduled date that is exactly 28 to 35 days after the system's current date.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard GNU Health client interface being navigated and used, rather than terminal commands, scripts, or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: The agent created actual records in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is entered into the correct form fields (e.g., Prescriptions, Lab Orders, Appointments) and saved, rather than just typed into a text editor, notepad, or search bar.

integrity.description: All medical actions were performed on the correct patient's record.
integrity.visual_evidence: The name 'John Zenon' is visible in the context, header, or patient field of the diagnosis, lab orders, prescriptions, and appointment screens.
